# Methodology / Theory / Analysis {#sec-methodology}

## 3.1 Functional Requirements

The following functional requirements (FR) define the core capabilities of the UpHeal system, derived from the problem definition in @sec-problem-definition and informed by the gap analysis in @sec-gap.

| ID | Requirement | Priority | Rationale |
|----|-------------|----------|-----------|
| FR-01 | The system shall allow users to register and authenticate via email/password and Google Sign-In. | Must | Secure identity management is a prerequisite for personalized, persistent interactions. |
| FR-02 | The system shall provide a conversational AI chatbot that generates responses conditioned on the user's detected emotional state and retrieved clinical context. | Must | Addresses the personalization gap identified in @sec-gap. |
| FR-03 | The system shall classify the user's emotional state from text input into one of six categories: Anxious, Sad, Angry, Neutral, Hopeful, or Distressed, with a confidence score. | Must | Real-time emotion adaptation is a core pillar of the system design. |
| FR-04 | The system shall detect crisis keywords in both English and Arabic and intervene with localized emergency resources when a crisis is identified. | Must | Bilingual crisis detection addresses the safety gap identified in @sec-gap. |
| FR-05 | The system shall retrieve clinically relevant passages from a vector database indexed with DSM-5-TR and CBT workbook content to ground LLM responses. | Must | RAG addresses the clinical grounding gap identified in @sec-gap. |
| FR-06 | The system shall generate GAD-7 and PHQ-9 clinical assessments and produce personalized 90-day wellness roadmaps based on the assessment results. | Must | Assessment-driven personalization is central to the therapeutic model. |
| FR-07 | The system shall implement a gamification engine with experience points, levels, badges, achievements, challenges, and streak tracking with freeze tokens. | Must | Gamification addresses the retention gap identified in @sec-gap. |
| FR-08 | The system shall provide journaling, mood tracking, sleep logging, and screen time monitoring features. | Must | These features provide the behavioral data needed for personalization and gamification. |
| FR-09 | The system shall support a community feature with a real-time feed, group chat, synchronized focus rooms, and user profiles. | Should | Community features leverage the relatedness dimension of SDT [@deci2000sdt] to sustain engagement. |
| FR-10 | The system shall screen every generated response through a clinical safety auditor that classifies it as GREEN, YELLOW, or RED before delivery. | Must | The clinical auditor ensures safety in every interaction, addressing the privacy and safety gap. |

## 3.2 Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-01 | Response latency | End-to-end response in under 4 seconds (target: 3.2 seconds) | Users expect near-real-time responses in conversational interfaces; latency above 4 seconds degrades perceived empathy. |
| NFR-02 | Availability | 99.5% uptime (planned maintenance windows excluded) | Mental health applications must be accessible during moments of crisis. |
| NFR-03 | Security | TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, row-level security (RLS), 3-tier JWT authentication | Sensitive mental health data requires defense in depth; regulatory compliance requires encryption at rest and in transit. |
| NFR-04 | Privacy | User data isolation via RLS policies; no cross-user data access | Each user's mental health data must be strictly isolated to prevent information leakage between accounts. |
| NFR-05 | Scalability | Handle 1,000 concurrent users with horizontal scaling | Sufficient for initial deployment; microservices architecture enables independent scaling of bottleneck services. |
| NFR-06 | Emotion classification accuracy | Minimum F1 score of 0.80 across all six categories | Clinical utility requires reliable emotion detection; below 0.80 the system risks misclassifying distress as neutral. |
| NFR-07 | Accessibility | Android 10+ and iOS 15+, minimum 4 GB RAM | Targeting the broadest possible mobile device compatibility in the MENA region. |

## 3.3 Use Case Diagram

Figure 3.1 presents the use case diagram for the UpHeal system, showing both actor use cases and system use cases.

```{python}
#| label: fig-use-case
#| fig-cap: "UpHeal use case diagram showing 8 user use cases and 6 system use cases"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 7))
ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 8)
ax.axis('off')

# Actors
user_box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((0.1, 3.5), 1.2, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.1", facecolor='#4C8BF5', edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.5)
ax.add_patch(user_box)
ax.text(0.7, 3.9, 'User', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', color='white')

system_box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((8.7, 3.5), 1.2, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.1", facecolor='#EA4335', edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.5)
ax.add_patch(system_box)
ax.text(9.3, 3.9, 'System', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', color='white')

# Use case positions
user_cases = [
    (2.5, 7.1, 'Register / Login'),
    (2.5, 6.2, 'Take Assessment'),
    (2.5, 5.3, 'Chat with AI Coach'),
    (2.5, 4.4, 'View Roadmap'),
    (2.5, 3.5, 'Track Mood'),
    (2.5, 2.6, 'Write Journal'),
    (2.5, 1.7, 'Manage Gamification'),
    (2.5, 0.8, 'Join Community'),
]

system_cases = [
    (6.5, 7.1, 'RAG Retrieval'),
    (6.5, 6.2, 'Emotion Classification'),
    (6.5, 5.3, 'Clinical Audit'),
    (6.5, 4.4, 'Crisis Detection'),
    (6.5, 3.5, 'Roadmap Generation'),
    (6.5, 2.6, 'Gamification XP Calculation'),
]

for x, y, label in user_cases:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 1.0, y - 0.32), 2.0, 0.64, boxstyle="round,pad=0.08", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

for x, y, label in system_cases:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 1.0, y - 0.32), 2.0, 0.64, boxstyle="round,pad=0.08", facecolor='#FCE8E6', edgecolor='#EA4335', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

# Actor -> use case arrows
for x, y, _ in user_cases:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x - 1.0, y), xytext=(1.3, 3.9),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#4C8BF5', lw=1.0))

# System -> use case arrows
for x, y, _ in system_cases:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x + 1.0, y), xytext=(8.7, 3.9),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#EA4335', lw=1.0))

# Include <<include>> dashed relationships
# Chat -> RAG, Emotion, Audit, Crisis
for sys_y in [7.1, 6.2, 5.3, 4.4]:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(6.5 - 1.0, sys_y), xytext=(2.5 + 1.0, 5.3),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=0.8, linestyle='dashed'))

# Assessment -> Roadmap Gen
ax.annotate('', xy=(6.5 - 1.0, 4.4), xytext=(2.5 + 1.0, 6.2),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=0.8, linestyle='dashed'))

# Mood, Journal, Gamification, Community -> XP Calculation
for src_y in [3.5, 2.6, 1.7, 0.8]:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(6.5 - 1.0, 2.6), xytext=(2.5 + 1.0, src_y),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=0.8, linestyle='dashed'))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

The use cases are organized into two categories. User use cases represent direct interactions: registration and authentication (FR-01), clinical assessment (FR-06), conversational AI chat (FR-02, FR-03), roadmap viewing (FR-06), mood tracking (FR-08), journaling (FR-08), gamification management (FR-07), and community participation (FR-09). System use cases represent internal processes triggered by user actions: RAG retrieval (FR-05), emotion classification (FR-03), clinical auditing (FR-10), crisis detection (FR-04), personalized roadmap generation (FR-06), and gamification XP calculation (FR-07).

## 3.4 System Architecture Overview

The UpHeal system follows a layered microservices architecture inspired by the RAG framework [@lewis2020rag] and modern LLM integration patterns [@toufique2023llm]. Figure 3.2 presents the high-level block diagram showing all components and their interconnections.

```{python}
#| label: fig-architecture
#| fig-cap: "UpHeal system architecture block diagram"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 10))
ax.set_xlim(0, 11)
ax.set_ylim(0, 10)
ax.axis('off')

# Layer backgrounds
layer_colors = ['#E3F2FD', '#E8F5E9', '#FFF3E0', '#F3E5F5', '#FCE4EC']
layer_labels = ['Client Layer', 'API Gateway Layer', 'Microservices Layer', 'AI Pipeline Layer', 'Data Layer']
layer_y = [9.0, 7.5, 5.0, 3.0, 1.0]
layer_h = [1.1, 1.1, 2.2, 1.3, 1.3]

for y, h, color, label in zip(layer_y, layer_h, layer_colors, layer_labels):
    rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((0.3, y - h/2), 10.4, h, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05", facecolor=color, edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.2, alpha=0.5)
    ax.add_patch(rect)
    ax.text(0.45, y + h/2 - 0.15, label, fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')

# Components as (x, y, label, color)
client = [(5.5, 9.0, 'Flutter Mobile App', '#4C8BF5')]

gateway = [
    (3.5, 7.5, 'FastAPI Gateway', '#34A853'),
    (5.5, 7.5, 'Auth Middleware', '#34A853'),
    (7.5, 7.5, 'Rate Limiter', '#34A853'),
]

microservices = [
    (2.0, 6.0, 'Chat\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (4.0, 6.0, 'Assessment\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (6.0, 6.0, 'Journal\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (8.0, 6.0, 'Telemetry\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (10.0, 6.0, 'Roadmap\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (2.0, 4.3, 'Knowledge Base\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (4.0, 4.3, 'Architect\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (6.0, 4.3, 'Ingestion\nService', '#FBBC05'),
    (8.0, 4.3, 'Auditor\nService', '#FBBC05'),
]

ai = [
    (2.5, 3.0, 'Emotion\nClassifier', '#9C27B0'),
    (5.0, 3.0, 'ChromaDB\nVector Store', '#9C27B0'),
    (7.5, 3.0, 'LLM\nService', '#9C27B0'),
    (10.0, 3.0, 'Clinical\nAuditor', '#9C27B0'),
]

data = [
    (3.5, 1.0, 'Supabase\nPostgreSQL', '#EA4335'),
    (5.5, 1.0, 'Supabase\nStorage', '#EA4335'),
    (7.5, 1.0, 'Supabase\nRealtime', '#EA4335'),
]

all_components = client + gateway + microservices + ai + data
for x, y, label, color in all_components:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.75, y - 0.45), 1.5, 0.9, boxstyle="round,pad=0.06", facecolor=color, edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.9)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7.5, color='white', fontweight='bold')

# Helper arrows
def draw_arrow(start, end, color='#555555', style='->', lw=1.0):
    ax.annotate('', xy=end, xytext=start, arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle=style, color=color, lw=lw))

# Client -> Gateway
draw_arrow((5.5, 8.55), (5.5, 7.95))

# Gateway -> Microservices
draw_arrow((5.5, 7.05), (5.5, 6.45))

# Microservices -> AI
draw_arrow((5.5, 3.9), (5.5, 3.45))

# AI -> Data
draw_arrow((5.5, 2.55), (5.5, 1.45))

# Internal gateway flow
draw_arrow((3.95, 7.5), (4.75, 7.5))
draw_arrow((6.25, 7.5), (6.75, 7.5))

# Chat service to AI components
for ax_pos in [2.5, 5.0, 7.5, 10.0]:
    draw_arrow((2.0, 5.55), (ax_pos, 3.45), '#666666', lw=0.8)

# Assessment -> DB
draw_arrow((4.0, 5.55), (3.5, 1.45), '#666666', lw=0.8)

# Ingestion -> ChromaDB
draw_arrow((6.0, 3.85), (5.0, 3.45), '#666666', lw=0.8)

# Realtime -> DB
draw_arrow((7.5, 0.55), (5.5, 0.55), '#666666', lw=0.8)

# Architect -> Assessment & Auditor
draw_arrow((3.25, 4.75), (4.0, 5.55), '#666666', lw=0.8)
draw_arrow((4.75, 4.75), (8.0, 5.55), '#666666', lw=0.8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Component Descriptions:**

| Layer | Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|------------|---------|
| Client | Flutter Mobile App | Flutter 3.24.5 | Cross-platform UI |
| Gateway | FastAPI Gateway | FastAPI 0.115+ | API routing, orchestration |
| Gateway | Auth Middleware | PyJWT + Supabase | 3-tier JWT verification |
| Gateway | Rate Limiter | SlowAPI | Request throttling |
| Microservices | Chat Service | Python + FastAPI | Conversational AI |
| Microservices | Assessment Service | Python + FastAPI | Clinical scoring |
| Microservices | Journal Service | Python + FastAPI | Journal CRUD |
| Microservices | Telemetry Service | Python + FastAPI | Usage analytics |
| Microservices | Roadmap Service | Python + FastAPI | Personalized roadmaps |
| Microservices | Knowledge Base Service | Python + ChromaDB | Vector store management |
| Microservices | Architect Service | Python + FastAPI | Assessment orchestration |
| Microservices | Ingestion Service | Python + pdfplumber | Document processing |
| Microservices | Auditor Service | Python + FastAPI | Safety validation |
| AI | Emotion Classifier | BERT/DistilBERT | Text emotion detection [@devlin2019bert] |
| AI | ChromaDB Vector Store | ChromaDB 0.5+ | Clinical knowledge retrieval |
| AI | LLM Service | Groq/Ollama | Response generation |
| AI | Clinical Auditor | Custom Python | Safety validation |
| Data | Supabase PostgreSQL | PostgreSQL 15+ | Relational data |
| Data | Supabase Storage | S3-compatible | Files, audio, images |
| Data | Supabase Realtime | WebSocket channels | Live community updates |

## 3.5 RAG Pipeline Design

### 3.5.1 Pipeline Architecture

```{python}
#| label: fig-rag-pipeline
#| fig-cap: "RAG pipeline block diagram"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
ax.set_xlim(0, 11)
ax.set_ylim(0, 5)
ax.axis('off')

# Indexing pipeline (top)
index_nodes = [
    (1.0, 3.8, 'Clinical\nDocuments'),
    (2.7, 3.8, 'PDF\nExtraction'),
    (4.4, 3.8, 'Semantic\nChunking'),
    (6.1, 3.8, 'Embedding\nGeneration'),
    (8.0, 3.8, 'ChromaDB\nIndex'),
]

# Query pipeline (bottom)
query_nodes = [
    (1.0, 1.3, 'User\nQuery'),
    (2.7, 1.3, 'Query\nEmbedding'),
    (4.4, 1.3, 'Vector\nSearch'),
    (6.1, 1.3, 'Top-k\nRetrieval'),
    (8.0, 1.3, 'Prompt\nAssembly'),
    (9.7, 1.3, 'LLM\nGeneration'),
    (11.0, 1.3, 'Response\nDelivery'),
]

# Draw indexing nodes
for x, y, label in index_nodes:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.6, y - 0.4), 1.2, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

# Draw query nodes
for x, y, label in query_nodes:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.55, y - 0.4), 1.1, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05", facecolor='#FFF3E0', edgecolor='#FBBC05', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

# Arrows in indexing pipeline
for i in range(len(index_nodes) - 1):
    x1, y1, _ = index_nodes[i]
    x2, y2, _ = index_nodes[i + 1]
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2 - 0.6, y1), xytext=(x1 + 0.6, y1),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#4C8BF5', lw=1.2))

# Arrows in query pipeline
for i in range(len(query_nodes) - 1):
    x1, y1, _ = query_nodes[i]
    x2, y2, _ = query_nodes[i + 1]
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2 - 0.55, y1), xytext=(x1 + 0.55, y1),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#FBBC05', lw=1.2))

# Connection from ChromaDB index to Vector Search
ax.annotate('', xy=(4.4, 1.7), xytext=(8.0, 3.4),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=1.0, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=0.3'))

# Add clinical audit step
ca_box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((9.7 - 0.55, 2.6 - 0.25), 1.1, 0.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#FCE8E6', edgecolor='#EA4335', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(ca_box)
ax.text(9.7, 2.6, 'Clinical\nAudit', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7)
ax.annotate('', xy=(9.7, 2.35), xytext=(9.7, 1.7),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#EA4335', lw=1.0))

# Labels for pipelines
ax.text(0.3, 3.8, 'Indexing', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='#4C8BF5', va='center')
ax.text(0.3, 1.3, 'Query', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='#FBBC05', va='center')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.5.2 Document Processing Algorithm

```
Algorithm: ProcessClinicalDocuments
Input: PDF_files[]
Output: Indexed_documents

1. For each PDF in PDF_files:
   a. Extract text using pdfplumber [@hutton2022pdfplumber]
   b. Filter noise (bibliography, headers, footers)
   c. Normalize Unicode
   
2. For each extracted text:
   a. Split into sentences using regex
   b. Detect topic shifts using all-MiniLM-L6-v2
   c. Create chunks with 15% overlap
   d. Label each chunk with difficulty, XP, tags
   
3. For each chunk:
   a. Generate embedding using all-mpnet-base-v2
   b. Store in ChromaDB with metadata
   
4. Return indexed collection
```

### 3.5.3 Retrieval Algorithm

```
Algorithm: RetrieveClinicalContext
Input: user_message, emotion_label, user_context
Output: top_k_passages

1. Embed user_message using all-mpnet-base-v2
2. Build metadata filter:
   a. Filter by symptom tags from user_context
   b. Filter by difficulty level
   c. Exclude high safety-risk tasks if user is in crisis
   
3. Query ChromaDB:
   a. Over-fetch: n = min(max(k * 4, 15), 50)
   b. Apply metadata filter
   c. Return results by cosine similarity
   
4. Apply digital detox boost:
   a. If user has high screen time, boost detox tasks
   b. If user has high social media ratio, penalize social tasks
   
5. Return top_k passages
```

### 3.5.4 Embedding Model Configuration

Table 3.1: Embedding model specifications.

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Model | all-mpnet-base-v2 |
| Dimensions | 768 |
| Similarity Metric | Cosine |
| HNSW ef_search | 128 |
| HNSW ef_construction | 200 |
| HNSW M | 16 |

## 3.6 LLM Integration

### 3.6.1 Prompt Assembly

The prompt is constructed from four components, following instruction-fine-tuning patterns [@chung2022scaling]:

```
Prompt Structure:
[SYSTEM] CBT therapist persona + safety rules
[CONTEXT] Retrieved clinical passages (top-k)
[EMOTION] Detected emotion label + confidence
[HISTORY] Last 5 conversation turns
[USER] Current user message
```

### 3.6.2 LLM Service Architecture

```{python}
#| label: fig-llm-service
#| fig-cap: "LLM service integration sequence diagram"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 7))
ax.set_xlim(0, 9)
ax.set_ylim(0, 7)
ax.axis('off')

participants = [
    (1.0, 'Chat\nService'),
    (3.0, 'RAG\nPipeline'),
    (5.0, 'Prompt\nAssembler'),
    (7.0, 'LLM\nService'),
    (9.0, 'Clinical\nAuditor'),
]

# Lifelines
for x, label in participants:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.5, 6.4), 1.0, 0.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, 6.65, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)
    ax.plot([x, x], [6.4, 0.8], color='#BBBBBB', linestyle='--', lw=1.0)

# Message arrows: (x1, x2, y, label, color)
messages = [
    (1.0, 3.0, 5.8, 'user_message + emotion', '#333333'),
    (3.0, 1.0, 5.2, 'top_k_passages', '#333333'),
    (1.0, 5.0, 4.6, 'passages + emotion + history', '#333333'),
    (5.0, 1.0, 4.0, 'structured_prompt', '#333333'),
    (1.0, 7.0, 3.4, 'structured_prompt', '#333333'),
    (7.0, 1.0, 2.8, 'generated_response', '#333333'),
    (1.0, 9.0, 2.2, 'response + user_locale', '#333333'),
    (9.0, 1.0, 1.6, 'safety_status', '#333333'),
]

for x1, x2, y, label, color in messages:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2, y), xytext=(x1, y),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color=color, lw=1.2))
    mid = (x1 + x2) / 2
    ax.text(mid, y + 0.12, label, ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=7)

# Alt block
alt_rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((0.5, 0.6), 2.0, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.03", facecolor='#E8F5E9', edgecolor='#34A853', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(alt_rect)
ax.text(0.65, 1.25, 'alt GREEN', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color='#34A853')
ax.text(1.5, 0.95, 'Deliver response', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

alt_rect2 = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((0.5, 0.0), 2.0, 0.6, boxstyle="round,pad=0.03", facecolor='#FCE8E6', edgecolor='#EA4335', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(alt_rect2)
ax.text(0.65, 0.45, 'else RED', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color='#EA4335')
ax.text(1.5, 0.2, 'Block + emergency resources', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.6.3 LLM Configuration

Table 3.2: LLM service configurations.

| Parameter | Groq (Current) | Ollama (Planned) |
|-----------|----------------|-------------------|
| Model | llama-3.1-8b-instant [@touvron2023llama; @chung2022scaling] | qwen2.5:7b / Gemma 2B [@team2024gemma] |
| Hosting | Cloud (Groq) | Self-hosted (RunPod) |
| Temperature | 0.7 | 0.7 |
| Max Tokens | 300 | 500 |
| Latency | ~2s | ~1s (GPU) |
| Cost | Per-request | Fixed (GPU rental) |

## 3.7 Emotion Classification

### 3.7.1 Classification Pipeline

```{python}
#| label: fig-emotion-pipeline
#| fig-cap: "Emotion classification pipeline"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 8))
ax.set_xlim(0, 6)
ax.set_ylim(0, 8)
ax.axis('off')

nodes = [
    (3.0, 7.2, 'User Input'),
    (3.0, 6.0, 'Text Preprocessing'),
    (3.0, 4.8, 'BERT Tokenizer'),
    (3.0, 3.6, 'BERT Encoder'),
    (3.0, 2.4, 'Classification Head'),
    (3.0, 1.2, 'Softmax'),
]

for x, y, label in nodes:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 1.2, y - 0.35), 2.4, 0.7, boxstyle="round,pad=0.06", facecolor='#F3E5F5', edgecolor='#9C27B0', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')

# Arrows down
for i in range(len(nodes) - 1):
    x1, y1, _ = nodes[i]
    x2, y2, _ = nodes[i + 1]
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2, y2 + 0.35), xytext=(x1, y1 - 0.35),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#9C27B0', lw=1.5))

# Split to outputs
ax.annotate('', xy=(1.5, 0.4), xytext=(3.0, 0.85),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#9C27B0', lw=1.2))
ax.annotate('', xy=(4.5, 0.4), xytext=(3.0, 0.85),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#9C27B0', lw=1.2))

out1 = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((0.6, 0.1), 1.8, 0.6, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F5E9', edgecolor='#34A853', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(out1)
ax.text(1.5, 0.4, 'Emotion Label', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

out2 = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((3.6, 0.1), 1.8, 0.6, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(out2)
ax.text(4.5, 0.4, 'Confidence\nScore', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.7.2 Emotion Categories

Table 3.3: Emotion classification categories.

| Emotion | ID | Description | Trigger Response |
|---------|-----|-------------|------------------|
| Anxious | 0 | Elevated worry, restlessness | Calming exercises, grounding |
| Sad | 1 | Low mood, hopelessness | Behavioral activation, CBT |
| Angry | 2 | Irritation, frustration | Anger management, breathing |
| Neutral | 3 | Balanced, calm | Standard therapeutic response |
| Hopeful | 4 | Optimistic, motivated | Goal-setting, reinforcement |
| Distressed | 5 | Severe emotional pain | Crisis protocol, immediate support |

### 3.7.3 Classification Algorithm

```
Algorithm: ClassifyEmotion
Input: user_text
Output: emotion_label, confidence_scores

1. Preprocess text:
   a. Lowercase
   b. Remove special characters
   c. Tokenize using BERT tokenizer
   
2. Forward pass through BERT:
   a. Input: token_ids + attention_mask
   b. Output: [CLS] token embedding
   
3. Classification:
   a. Linear layer: 768 → 6
   b. Softmax: probability distribution
   
4. Return:
   a. Argmax: emotion_label
   b. All probabilities: confidence_scores
```

> **Design vs. implementation note:** Section 3.7 describes the designed emotion classification pipeline. The current production implementation uses keyword-based sentiment analysis with clinical safety keywords within the auditor service, while the BERT-based classifier remains planned for future deployment.

## 3.8 Gamification System

### 3.8.1 Gamification Architecture

```{python}
#| label: fig-gamification-state
#| fig-cap: "Gamification state machine"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 7))
ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 7)
ax.axis('off')

# State nodes arranged in two columns
states = [
    (2.5, 6.2, 'Idle'),
    (2.5, 5.2, 'Activity\nDetected'),
    (2.5, 4.2, 'XP\nCalculated'),
    (2.5, 3.2, 'Streak\nChecked'),
    (2.5, 2.2, 'Multiplier\nApplied'),
    (2.5, 1.2, 'Level\nChecked'),
    (5.0, 1.2, 'Badge\nChecked'),
    (5.0, 2.2, 'Achievement\nChecked'),
    (5.0, 3.2, 'Reward\nDisplayed'),
]

# Special branch nodes
branch = [
    (7.5, 2.2, 'Level Up'),
    (7.5, 1.2, 'Badge\nUnlocked'),
]

for x, y, label in states:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.6, y - 0.35), 1.2, 0.7, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05", facecolor='#FFF3E0', edgecolor='#FBBC05', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

for x, y, label in branch:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.6, y - 0.35), 1.2, 0.7, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05", facecolor='#E8F5E9', edgecolor='#34A853', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

# Start node
start = plt.Circle((2.5, 6.85), 0.12, color='#333333')
ax.add_patch(start)
ax.annotate('', xy=(2.5, 6.55), xytext=(2.5, 6.73),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#333333', lw=1.2))

# Main flow arrows (down)
main_flow = [
    (2.5, 5.85, 2.5, 5.55),
    (2.5, 4.85, 2.5, 4.55),
    (2.5, 3.85, 2.5, 3.55),
    (2.5, 2.85, 2.5, 2.55),
    (2.5, 1.85, 2.5, 1.55),
    (3.1, 1.2, 4.4, 1.2),
    (5.0, 0.85, 5.0, 0.55),
    (5.0, 0.55, 2.5, 0.55),
    (2.5, 0.55, 2.5, 5.85),
]

for x1, y1, x2, y2 in main_flow:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2, y2), xytext=(x1, y1),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#FBBC05', lw=1.2))

# Horizontal arrows
ax.annotate('', xy=(4.4, 2.2), xytext=(3.1, 2.2),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#FBBC05', lw=1.2))
ax.annotate('', xy=(4.4, 3.2), xytext=(3.1, 3.2),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#FBBC05', lw=1.2))

# Branch arrows
ax.annotate('', xy=(6.9, 2.2), xytext=(3.1, 1.85),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=1.0, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=0.2'))
ax.annotate('', xy=(6.9, 1.2), xytext=(3.1, 1.55),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=1.0, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=-0.2'))

# Return from branches to Reward Displayed
ax.annotate('', xy=(6.9, 3.2), xytext=(7.5, 2.55),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=1.0))
ax.annotate('', xy=(6.9, 3.2), xytext=(7.5, 1.55),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=1.0))

# Labels
ax.text(3.4, 5.7, 'user performs action', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(3.4, 4.7, 'calculate base XP', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(3.4, 3.7, 'check streak', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(3.4, 2.7, 'apply multiplier', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(3.4, 1.7, 'check level up', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(6.0, 1.5, 'check badge', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')
ax.text(6.0, 2.5, 'check achievement', fontsize=7, style='italic', color='#666666')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.8.2 XP Calculation Formula

```
XP_total = Base_XP × Streak_Multiplier × Difficulty_Multiplier

Where:
- Base_XP: Activity-specific value (5-15)
- Streak_Multiplier: 1.0× to 5.0× based on streak length
- Difficulty_Multiplier: 1.0× to 2.0× based on task difficulty
```

### 3.8.3 Level Thresholds

Table 3.4: Level progression thresholds.

| Level | XP Required | Total XP | Unlocks |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------|
| 1 | 0 | 0 | Basic features |
| 2 | 100 | 100 | First badge slot |
| 3 | 150 | 250 | Journal templates |
| 4 | 200 | 450 | Advanced challenges |
| 5 | 250 | 700 | Community access |
| 10 | 500 | 2,450 | Premium features |

## 3.9 Security Architecture

### 3.9.1 Authentication Flow

```{python}
#| label: fig-auth-flow
#| fig-cap: "JWT authentication flow"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
ax.set_xlim(0, 8)
ax.set_ylim(0, 6)
ax.axis('off')

participants = [
    (1.0, 'User'),
    (3.0, 'Flutter\nApp'),
    (5.0, 'Gateway'),
    (7.0, 'Supabase'),
]

for x, label in participants:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.55, 5.4), 1.1, 0.45, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.2)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, 5.62, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
    ax.plot([x, x], [5.4, 0.7], color='#BBBBBB', linestyle='--', lw=1.0)

messages = [
    (1.0, 3.0, 4.9, 'Email + Password', '#333333'),
    (3.0, 7.0, 4.3, 'signInWithPassword()', '#333333'),
    (7.0, 3.0, 3.7, 'JWT Token', '#333333'),
    (3.0, 5.0, 3.1, 'Request + Bearer Token', '#333333'),
    (5.0, 5.0, 2.3, 'Verify JWT', '#333333'),
    (5.0, 3.0, 1.5, 'Success + Data', '#34A853'),
]

for x1, x2, y, label, color in messages:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2, y), xytext=(x1, y),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color=color, lw=1.2))
    mid = (x1 + x2) / 2
    ax.text(mid, y + 0.12, label, ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=8)

# Alt block
alt_rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((4.4, 0.7), 2.2, 1.0, boxstyle="round,pad=0.03", facecolor='#E8F5E9', edgecolor='#34A853', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(alt_rect)
ax.text(4.55, 1.55, 'alt Valid', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color='#34A853')
ax.text(5.5, 1.1, 'Return success + data', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

alt_rect2 = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((1.8, 0.7), 2.2, 0.7, boxstyle="round,pad=0.03", facecolor='#FCE8E6', edgecolor='#EA4335', linewidth=1.0)
ax.add_patch(alt_rect2)
ax.text(1.95, 1.25, 'else Invalid', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color='#EA4335')
ax.text(2.9, 0.95, 'Return 401 Unauthorized', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.9.2 Security Layers

Table 3.5: Security measures by layer.

| Layer | Mechanism | Implementation |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| Network | TLS 1.3 | All API connections |
| Storage | AES-256 | Supabase at-rest encryption |
| Application | JWT | HS256 + ES256 + JWKS |
| Database | RLS | Row-Level Security policies |
| API | Rate Limiting | 10 req/min on /api/assess |
| Crisis | Keyword Detection | Bilingual (EN/AR) |

### 3.9.3 Clinical Safety Audit

```
Algorithm: ClinicalAudit
Input: response_text, user_locale
Output: safety_status, emergency_payload

1. Crisis Detection:
   a. Scan for crisis keywords (EN/AR)
   b. If found: return RED + hotlines
   
2. Robotic Tone Detection:
   a. Check for clinical/dismissive language
   b. If detected: flag for revision
   
3. Safety Risk Assessment:
   a. Check if response contains risky advice
   b. If found: return YELLOW + advisory
   
4. Frustration Detection:
   a. Analyze user message sentiment
   b. If frustrated: add empathy boost
   
5. If all checks pass:
   a. Return GREEN
```

## 3.10 Data Flow Integration

### 3.10.1 End-to-End Data Flow

```{python}
#| label: fig-data-flow
#| fig-cap: "Complete data flow for a chat request"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 9))
ax.set_xlim(0, 7)
ax.set_ylim(0, 9)
ax.axis('off')

# Nodes: (x, y, label, type, color)
# type: 'box' or 'diamond'
nodes = [
    (3.5, 8.5, 'User sends\nmessage', 'box', '#4C8BF5'),
    (3.5, 7.6, 'Flutter App', 'box', '#E8F0FE'),
    (3.5, 6.7, 'FastAPI Gateway', 'box', '#34A853'),
    (3.5, 5.8, 'Auth Valid?', 'diamond', '#FFF3E0'),
    (1.2, 5.0, '401 Error', 'box', '#FCE8E6'),
    (3.5, 5.0, 'Chat Service', 'box', '#FBBC05'),
    (1.2, 4.0, 'Emotion\nClassifier', 'box', '#9C27B0'),
    (1.2, 3.1, 'Emotion:\nAnxious, 0.85', 'box', '#F3E5F5'),
    (3.5, 4.0, 'ChromaDB\nRetrieval', 'box', '#9C27B0'),
    (3.5, 3.1, 'Top-5\npassages', 'box', '#F3E5F5'),
    (5.8, 4.0, 'Prompt\nAssembler', 'box', '#9C27B0'),
    (5.8, 3.1, 'LLM\nService', 'box', '#9C27B0'),
    (5.8, 2.2, 'Generated\nresponse', 'box', '#F3E5F5'),
    (3.5, 2.0, 'Clinical\nAuditor', 'box', '#EA4335'),
    (3.5, 1.1, 'Safety\nCheck', 'diamond', '#FFF3E0'),
    (1.5, 0.3, 'Emergency\nresources', 'box', '#FCE8E6'),
    (5.5, 0.3, 'Deliver\nresponse', 'box', '#E8F5E9'),
    (3.5, -0.3, 'Store in\nSupabase', 'box', '#EA4335'),
    (3.5, -1.1, 'Return to\nUser', 'box', '#4C8BF5'),
]

for x, y, label, ntype, color in nodes:
    if ntype == 'box':
        box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.65, y - 0.32), 1.3, 0.64, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor=color, edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.9)
        ax.add_patch(box)
        text_color = 'white' if color in ['#4C8BF5', '#34A853', '#9C27B0', '#EA4335', '#FBBC05'] else '#333333'
    else:  # diamond
        diamond = mpatches.RegularPolygon((x, y), numVertices=4, radius=0.55, orientation=0.785, facecolor=color, edgecolor='#333333', linewidth=1.0)
        ax.add_patch(diamond)
        text_color = '#333333'
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color=text_color, fontweight='bold')

# Arrows
arrows = [
    (3.5, 8.18, 3.5, 7.92),
    (3.5, 7.28, 3.5, 7.02),
    (3.5, 6.38, 3.5, 6.12),
    (3.5, 5.47, 1.2, 5.32),
    (1.2, 4.68, 1.2, 4.32),
    (1.2, 3.78, 1.2, 3.42),
    (3.5, 5.47, 3.5, 5.32),
    (3.5, 4.68, 3.5, 4.32),
    (3.5, 3.78, 3.5, 3.42),
    (3.5, 4.68, 5.15, 4.15),
    (5.8, 3.68, 5.8, 3.42),
    (5.8, 2.78, 5.8, 2.52),
    (5.8, 1.88, 3.85, 1.85),
    (1.2, 2.78, 3.15, 1.85),
    (3.5, 1.68, 3.5, 1.42),
    (3.5, 0.78, 1.5, 0.62),
    (3.5, 0.78, 5.5, 0.62),
    (1.5, 0.0, 3.15, -0.15),
    (5.5, 0.0, 3.85, -0.15),
    (3.5, -0.62, 3.5, -0.78),
]

for x1, y1, x2, y2 in arrows:
    ax.annotate('', xy=(x2, y2), xytext=(x1, y1),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#555555', lw=1.0))

# Decision labels
ax.text(3.85, 5.7, 'No', fontsize=8, color='#EA4335', fontweight='bold')
ax.text(3.85, 5.35, 'Yes', fontsize=8, color='#34A853', fontweight='bold')
ax.text(3.85, 1.2, 'RED', fontsize=8, color='#EA4335', fontweight='bold')
ax.text(3.85, 0.8, 'GREEN', fontsize=8, color='#34A853', fontweight='bold')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 3.10.2 Integration Points

Table 3.6: Service integration matrix.

| Service | Depends On | Provides To |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Chat Service | Emotion, RAG, LLM, Auditor | Flutter App |
| Emotion Classifier | BERT Model | Chat Service |
| RAG Pipeline | ChromaDB, Embeddings | Chat Service, Architect |
| LLM Service | Groq/Ollama | Chat Service |
| Clinical Auditor | Keyword DB, Sentiment | Chat Service |
| Assessment | Supabase | Flutter App, Architect |
| Journal | Supabase | Flutter App |
| Architect | Assessment, Auditor | Gateway (roadmap generation) |
| Ingestion | pdfplumber, Embeddings | Knowledge Base |
| Knowledge Base | ChromaDB | RAG Pipeline |
| Roadmap | Supabase, Architect | Flutter App |
| Telemetry | Supabase | Flutter App |

### 3.10.3 Data Flow Narrative

When a user sends a message, the system processes it through the following integrated pipeline:

1. **Authentication**: The Flutter app sends the message with a JWT token to the FastAPI gateway. The auth middleware verifies the token using Supabase's public key.

2. **Emotion Detection**: The chat service forwards the message to the emotion classifier. The BERT-based model tokenizes the text, encodes it through 12 transformer layers, and outputs a probability distribution across six emotion categories. The label with the highest probability (e.g., "Anxious" with 85% confidence) is returned.

3. **Context Retrieval**: The chat service queries the RAG pipeline. The user's message is embedded using `all-mpnet-base-v2` (768 dimensions). The vector is compared against the ChromaDB index using HNSW approximate nearest neighbor search. The top-5 most relevant passages are retrieved, filtered by the user's clinical profile (symptom tags, difficulty level), and boosted for digital detox if screen time is high.

4. **Response Generation**: The retrieved passages, detected emotion, and conversation history (last 5 turns) are assembled into a structured prompt. The LLM service (Groq Cloud's `llama-3.1-8b-instant`) generates a response conditioned on this prompt.

5. **Safety Audit**: The generated response is passed to the clinical auditor. The auditor scans for crisis keywords (in both English and Arabic), detects robotic tone, flags safety risks, and scores user frustration. If the response passes all checks (GREEN status), it is delivered. If a crisis is detected (RED status), the response is blocked and replaced with emergency resources.

6. **Persistence**: The conversation turn (user message, response, emotion label, safety status) is stored in Supabase PostgreSQL. The gamification engine calculates XP based on the activity type, streak length, and difficulty multiplier.

7. **Delivery**: The Flutter app receives the response, displays it with an emotion badge, and triggers any reward animations (XP burst, level-up, badge unlock) if thresholds are met.

This flow completes in approximately 3.2 seconds, with 2.2 seconds consumed by LLM generation and the remaining 1.0 seconds distributed across authentication, emotion classification, retrieval, auditing, and persistence.

## 3.11 Implementation Stack

Table 3.7: Complete technology stack.

| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|---------|
| Frontend | Flutter | 3.24.5 | Cross-platform UI |
| Navigation | GoRouter | 14.6.2 | Declarative routing |
| State Mgmt | Provider | 6.1.1 | ChangeNotifier |
| Backend | FastAPI | 0.115+ | REST API gateway |
| ASGI | Uvicorn | 0.32+ | Async server |
| Auth | Supabase Auth | 2.12+ | JWT + OAuth2 |
| Database | PostgreSQL | 15+ | Relational data |
| Realtime | Supabase Realtime | - | WebSocket channels |
| Vector DB | ChromaDB | 0.5+ | HNSW search |
| Embedding | Sentence-Transformers | 2.2+ | all-mpnet-base-v2 |
| Chat LLM | Groq API | - | llama-3.1-8b-instant |
| Planned LLM | Ollama | - | qwen2.5:7b / Gemma |
| PDF | pdfplumber | 0.10+ | Text extraction |
| Community | Supabase Edge Functions | - | Serverless logic |
| Container | Docker | 24+ | Deployment |
| Hosting | DigitalOcean | - | Backend server |
| GPU | RunPod | - | A100 80GB (planned) |

The frontend is built with Flutter [@flutter2024], the API gateway with FastAPI [@fastapi2024], and authentication, database, and real-time services are provided by Supabase [@supabase2024].

## 3.12 Hardware Specifications

Table 3.8: Infrastructure specifications.

| Component | Specification | Purpose |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| Backend Server | DigitalOcean 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM | FastAPI gateway |
| Database | Supabase managed | PostgreSQL + Storage |
| Vector Store | ChromaDB persistent | Clinical knowledge |
| Embedding | CPU inference | all-mpnet-base-v2 |
| Chat LLM | Groq Cloud | Production inference |
| Planned GPU | RunPod A100 80GB | Self-hosted LLM |
| Client | Android 10+ / iOS 15+ | 4GB RAM minimum |

## 3.13 Data Models

### 3.13.1 Core Application Tables

The system uses 12 core tables in Supabase PostgreSQL to support the primary therapeutic workflow.

Table 3.9: Core application data models.

| Table | Primary Key | Key Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------------|------------|---------|
| profiles | id (UUID) | display_name, level, xp_total, streak_count, freeze_tokens | User profile and gamification state |
| chat_sessions | id (UUID) | user_id, roadmap_id, created_at | Chat session metadata |
| chat_messages | id (UUID) | session_id, role, content, emotion_label, safety_status, created_at | Conversation messages |
| assessments | id (UUID) | user_id, assessment_type, score, severity, created_at | Clinical assessment records |
| journal_entries | id (UUID) | user_id, title, content, mood_rating, created_at | User journaling |
| mood_logs | id (UUID) | user_id, mood_score, emotion_label, logged_at | Mood tracking |
| sleep_logs | id (UUID) | user_id, duration_minutes, quality, logged_at | Sleep tracking |
| roadmaps | id (UUID) | user_id, overview, suggested_tasks, status | Personalized roadmaps |
| badges | id (UUID) | user_id, badge_id, earned_at | Earned badges |
| achievements | id (UUID) | user_id, achievement_id, progress, unlocked_at | Achievement tracking |
| challenges | id (UUID) | user_id, challenge_id, progress, expires_at | Active challenges |
| crisis_reports | id (UUID) | user_id, trigger_text, action_taken, created_at | Safety audit log |

### 3.13.2 Community Tables

Thirteen tables implement the community feature data model, supporting groups, posts, comments, real-time channels, moderation, and notifications.

Table 3.10: Community data models.

| Table | Primary Key | Key Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------------|------------|---------|
| community_groups | id (UUID) | name, description, type, created_by | Support groups and communities |
| community_group_members | id (UUID) | group_id, user_id, role, joined_at | Membership with role-based access |
| community_posts | id (UUID) | group_id, author_id, title, content, created_at | Group posts and announcements |
| community_comments | id (UUID) | post_id, author_id, content, created_at | Post comments |
| community_likes | id (UUID) | target_id, user_id, target_type, created_at | Likes on posts/comments |
| community_reports | id (UUID) | reporter_id, target_id, reason, status, created_at | Content moderation reports |
| community_messages | id (UUID) | channel_id, author_id, content, created_at | Group chat messages |
| community_channels | id (UUID) | group_id, name, type, created_at | Chat channels within groups |
| community_notifications | id (UUID) | user_id, type, payload, read, created_at | User notifications |
| community_banned_users | id (UUID) | group_id, user_id, reason, banned_at | Group-level user bans |
| community_moderation_log | id (UUID) | moderator_id, action, target_id, created_at | Moderator action history |
| community_user_profiles | id (UUID) | user_id, display_name, avatar_url, bio | Community-specific profiles |
| community_pinned_posts | id (UUID) | post_id, group_id, pinned_by, pinned_at | Pinned announcements |

### 3.13.3 Entity Relationships

Figure 3.9 shows the primary entity relationships between core and community tables. Each core table is linked to `profiles` through `user_id` with row-level security enforcing user isolation. Community tables form a separate subgraph anchored on `community_groups`, with `community_group_members` bridging users to groups.

```{python}
#| label: fig-er-diagram
#| fig-cap: "Entity relationship diagram for core and community tables"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 8))
ax.set_xlim(0, 12)
ax.set_ylim(0, 8)
ax.axis('off')

# Title area
ax.text(0.3, 7.6, 'Core Tables', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#4C8BF5')
ax.text(7.3, 7.6, 'Community Tables', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#34A853')

# Core tables (left column)
core = [
    (2.0, 7.0, 'profiles'),
    (1.0, 5.8, 'assessments'),
    (3.0, 5.8, 'chat_sessions'),
    (1.0, 4.8, 'journal_entries'),
    (3.0, 4.8, 'chat_messages'),
    (1.0, 3.8, 'mood_logs'),
    (3.0, 3.8, 'roadmaps'),
    (1.0, 2.8, 'sleep_logs'),
    (3.0, 2.8, 'badges'),
    (1.0, 1.8, 'achievements'),
    (3.0, 1.8, 'challenges'),
    (2.0, 0.8, 'crisis_reports'),
]

# Community tables (right column)
community = [
    (8.5, 7.0, 'community_groups'),
    (7.0, 5.9, 'community_user_profiles'),
    (10.0, 5.9, 'community_group_members'),
    (8.5, 4.8, 'community_channels'),
    (7.0, 4.0, 'community_posts'),
    (10.0, 4.0, 'community_messages'),
    (7.0, 3.0, 'community_comments'),
    (10.0, 3.0, 'community_likes'),
    (7.0, 2.0, 'community_reports'),
    (10.0, 2.0, 'community_banned_users'),
    (7.0, 1.0, 'community_moderation_log'),
    (10.0, 1.0, 'community_pinned_posts'),
    (8.5, 0.2, 'community_notifications'),
]

for x, y, label in core:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.75, y - 0.25), 1.5, 0.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F0FE', edgecolor='#4C8BF5', linewidth=1.0)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7.5)

for x, y, label in community:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x - 0.85, y - 0.25), 1.7, 0.5, boxstyle="round,pad=0.04", facecolor='#E8F5E9', edgecolor='#34A853', linewidth=1.0)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x, y, label, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7)

# Relationships: profiles -> core tables
for _, y, _ in core[1:]:
    ax.plot([2.0, 2.0], [6.75, y + 0.25], color='#4C8BF5', lw=0.8)

# profiles -> community_user_profiles
ax.annotate('', xy=(7.0, 5.9), xytext=(2.75, 7.0),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=0.8, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=0.2'))

# community_groups -> members, channels, posts
for target in [(10.0, 5.9), (8.5, 4.8), (7.0, 4.0)]:
    ax.annotate('', xy=target, xytext=(8.5, 6.75),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=0.8))

# channels -> messages
ax.annotate('', xy=(10.0, 4.0), xytext=(8.5, 4.55),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=0.8))

# posts -> comments, likes, reports, pinned_posts
for target in [(7.0, 3.0), (10.0, 3.0), (7.0, 2.0), (10.0, 1.0)]:
    ax.annotate('', xy=target, xytext=(7.0, 3.75),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#34A853', lw=0.8))

# Legend
legend_items = [
    (0.3, 0.1, '#E8F0FE', '#4C8BF5', 'Core table'),
    (2.5, 0.1, '#E8F5E9', '#34A853', 'Community table'),
]
for x, y, fc, ec, label in legend_items:
    box = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch((x, y - 0.1), 0.3, 0.2, boxstyle="round,pad=0.02", facecolor=fc, edgecolor=ec, linewidth=1.0)
    ax.add_patch(box)
    ax.text(x + 0.4, y, label, va='center', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
